etymology.description: Depicts an agricultural tool for tilling fields, possibly made from a large clam 蜃(蜃) (shèn, 'large clam')
etymology.interpretation: 辰(辰) (chén, 'field') represents an ancient tool used in agriculture, highlighting its cultural significance in early farming practices.

examples.chinese: 他在辰里播种(他在辰裡播種)。
examples.english: He sows seeds in the field.

examples.chinese: 黎明破晓时(黎明破曉時)，农夫已走进辰中(農夫已走進辰中)。
examples.pinyin: Límíng pòxiǎo shí, nóngfū yǐ zǒu jìn chén zhōng.
examples.english: At daybreak, the farmer has already entered the field.

examples.chinese: 暴雨过后(暴雨過後)，辰边生长出新绿(辰邊生長出新綠)。
examples.pinyin: Bàoyǔ guòhòu, chén biān shēngzhǎng chū xīn lǜ.
examples.english: After the heavy rain, new green grows by the field.
